title: Dungeon Reflection Grading Suite
description: A dungeon environment surrounds a reflective pool with a ShaderBall floating above it. The user progressively layers post-processing effects — bloom, color grading, vignette, chromatic aberration, film grain, and depth of field — tuning each with validated parameters to transform the raw scene into a cinematic dungeon atmosphere.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with EffectComposer from three/addons. Load three GLB assets: place dungeon_warkarma.glb as the surrounding environment scaled to fill the scene, position pool.glb at ground level centered in the dungeon, and float ShaderBall.glb 1.5 units above the pool, slowly rotating on its Y-axis. Set up a perspective camera at position (0, 3, 8) looking at the pool center. Add dim ambient light (intensity 0.15, color #2a2a4a) and two point lights: one warm orange (intensity 2, positioned above the ShaderBall) and one cool blue (intensity 1.5, positioned behind the pool) to create dramatic dungeon lighting. Build an EffectComposer pipeline with RenderPass as the base. Create a GUI panel (use lil-gui from CDN) with six sequential steps the user must configure: Step 1 — Bloom: add UnrealBloomPass, expose threshold (range 0.0-1.0, must be set between 0.1-0.5), strength (0.0-3.0, must be 0.8-2.0), and radius (0.0-1.0, must be 0.2-0.7). Step 2 — Color Grading: add a custom ShaderPass that applies lift-gamma-gain; expose gamma (0.5-2.0, valid range 0.7-1.4) and gain (0.5-2.0, valid range 0.9-1.6). Step 3 — Vignette: add a ShaderPass with vignette shader; expose darkness (0.0-2.0, valid 0.5-1.5) and offset (0.0-2.0, valid 0.5-1.2). Step 4 — Chromatic Aberration: add a custom ShaderPass offsetting RGB channels; expose intensity (0.0-0.02, valid 0.002-0.012). Step 5 — Film Grain: add a ShaderPass with animated noise; expose grain intensity (0.0-0.5, valid 0.05-0.3). Step 6 — Depth of Field: add BokehPass focused on the ShaderBall; expose focus (1.0-20.0, valid 4.0-10.0), aperture (0.0-0.1, valid 0.01-0.06), and maxblur (0.0-0.02, valid 0.003-0.015). Each step has a checkbox to enable/disable. Add a 'Validate' button that checks all six effects are enabled and all parameters fall within their valid ranges, displaying a green success overlay or red error messages listing which parameters are out of range. Include OrbitControls for camera interaction. The pool should show a simple planar reflection of the ShaderBall using a MeshStandardMaterial with envMap or a reflective plane. Animate the ShaderBall bobbing gently up and down with a sine wave.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  bloomThreshold: number,         // bloom effect threshold value, valid range 0.1-0.5
  bloomStrength: number,         // bloom effect strength value, valid range 0.8-2.0
  bloomRadius: number,         // bloom effect radius value, valid range 0.2-0.7
  colorGamma: number,         // color grading gamma value, valid range 0.7-1.4
  colorGain: number,         // color grading gain value, valid range 0.9-1.6
  vignetteIntensity: number,         // vignette darkness intensity, valid range 0.2-0.8
  chromaticAberration: number,         // chromatic aberration offset amount, valid range 0.001-0.01
  filmGrainIntensity: number,         // film grain noise intensity, valid range 0.05-0.25
  dofFocusDistance: number,         // depth of field focus distance in units, valid range 6.0-10.0
  dofAperture: number,         // depth of field aperture size, valid range 0.001-0.01
  effectsConfigured: number,         // count of effects properly configured (0-6)
  shaderBallRotation: number,         // current Y-axis rotation of ShaderBall in radians
}
